source: pred.gg public profile + own aggregate baselines

archetype.label: The True Flex
archetype.receipt: 5 roles at 100+ games, all within 2.5 wins per 100 of each other — genuinely fill-proof.

entries.change: Move the offlane games to jungle

entries.receipt: 52.0% over 166 games on Zinx vs 50.5% overall

entries.change: Queue jungle instead of the usual mix
entries.receipt: 51.0% there vs 50.5% overall, 157 games of evidence

ledger.note: wins per 100 games spent on each change; entries overlap and do not sum

leanInto.name: Zinx
leanInto.engineCoachLine: Sim-optimal for heal/shield output: first spike lands around minute 15 (Oblivion Crown), third item by minute 28. Take Vesh: +13.5% on the damage rotation at minute 15.

plan: Queue jungle as primary. It is Willy's best role at 51.0% over 157 games (overall is 50.5%). Set support as secondary, not the other way around.
Comfort-hero rule for the climb: Zinx. Compared with the average player on the same hero, Willy wins about 2.5 more games per 100 on it.
Willy's champion pool is 12+ heroes wide and the top three are only 47% of the games. Gold-to-Platinum climbs are almost always pool-narrowing stories: aim for 70%+ of games on the top three.
The math: Willy is at 444 VP; Platinum III starts at 900. The all-time peak is 903 — Platinum-level rating has been touched once already; this is a consistency problem, not a ceiling problem.
honesty: personal winrates are shrunk toward the player's own average (small heater samples do not count as mains)
field baselines are all-ranks aggregates from our current-patch match sample, not Gold-bracket-specific yet
winrate edges are observational; they say where the player wins, not why

insights.title: Cuban brings out Willy's best
insights.finding: Willy's record alongside Cuban Noobster is 54% over 401 ranked games — the strongest pairing in the stack. When only two of the squad queue, make it this pair.

insights.title: Gadget is the long-term relationship
insights.finding: 224 games on Gadget at 50.5% — the field averages 50.6%. The comfort is real; the edge is roughly neutral.

insights.title: For the group chat
insights.finding: 1 pentakill in real 5v5s and a longest spree of 23 (plus 1 more in ARAM and brawls — the group chat can rule on whether those count). Willy has receipts.

insights.title: Willy punches back after losses
insights.finding: Willy wins MORE right after a loss (70%) than after a win (30%). Tilt is not the problem; complacency might be.
insights.receipt: 7/10 after losses vs 3/10 after wins, last 21 games

coachReasoning.assessment: Willy's recent games swing entirely on deaths: the two Drongo carry games split 8/0/3 in the win against 5/9/3 in the loss (9 deaths vs the 3 norm, +200%), and the Gadget mid loss ran 5 deaths against a 3 norm. The biggest lever is not a new hero or queue — it is playing the damage roles from the range that won the clean Drongo game.
plan: On Drongo, replay the 8/0/3 win, not the 5/9/3 loss: sit at maximum gun range behind the frontline, save the knockback as an exit instead of an opener, and leave the fight the moment the frontline falls.

On Gadget, poke from behind the minion wave and only step forward after the enemy has burned their gap-closer — the recent mid loss ran 5 deaths against the 3 norm because trades kept happening inside enemy reach.

On Zinx, build toward the minute-15 Oblivion Crown spike with Vesh (+13.5% on the damage rotation at minute 15), and call a grouped fight right when it lands; the third item comes online around minute 28, so drag even games toward that window.

On Shinbi, keep the 8/1/3 hit-and-run pattern: dash through the wave to trade and dash back out — the mitigation number from that win (605/min against the 876 norm) says the wins come from skirmishing, not from standing and soaking.
When filling, send the offlane games to jungle (48.6% there vs 51.0%; worth +2.5 wins per 100 shifted) and bring the same skirmish-and-exit habits from the Shinbi win along.

insights.title: Deaths decide the game
insights.finding: The Drongo pair says it plainly: 0 deaths won 8/0/3, and 9 deaths (vs the 3 norm, +200%) lost 5/9/3 — same hero, same role, spacing was the only difference.

insights.title: Zinx is the climb hero
insights.finding: 56.6% over 166 games, and the kit math agrees: heal/shield output spikes at minute 15 with Oblivion Crown, and Vesh adds +13.5% to the damage rotation right there.

insights.title: Duo with Cuban
insights.finding: Willy's record alongside Cuban Noobster is 54% over 401 ranked games — the strongest pairing in the stack. When only two of the squad queue, make it this pair.

insights.title: The danger game is after a win
insights.finding: Willy wins 70% right after a loss versus 30% after a win — tilt is not the leak; the loose game after a victory is.

coachReasoningSource: in-session pred-scout-coach agent, grounded in the report + the player’s recent reviewed games; every number ground-checked, failing lines dropped
